---
title: "Clean ALL plots with brief summary"
subtitle: "An R-driven workflow"
author: "Jacqueline Wu"
date: "2025-10-19"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    embed-resources: true
---

```{r}
#| label: setup
#| include: false
options(repos = c(CRAN = "https://cloud.r-project.org"))

# install only if missing, then load
needed <- c("vroom","dplyr","readr","stringr","fs","ggplot2","tidyr","readxl")
to_install <- setdiff(needed, rownames(installed.packages()))
if (length(to_install)) install.packages(to_install, dependencies = TRUE, quiet = TRUE)
invisible(lapply(needed, library, character.only = TRUE))
```
#### load the dataframe
```{r}
in_path  <- "E:/merge_all/Outputs/Ellsworth/clean_Ellsworth_ALL.csv"
out_path <- sub("\\.csv$", "_clean.csv", in_path)

df <- vroom::vroom(in_path, show_col_types = FALSE)
# check df
class(df)
dim(df)
str(df, max.level = 1)
```
#### remove Abiotic predictions and clean the dataframe
```{r}
df <- df %>% dplyr::rename(common_name = `Common name`)
n_before <- nrow(df)
df <- df %>% 
  dplyr::filter(`Scientific name` != "Abiotic")
df <- df %>% 
  dplyr::filter(`common_name` != "Biotic Insect")
df <- df %>% 
  dplyr:: filter(`common_name` != "Biotic Anuran")
n_removed <- n_before - nrow(df)
n_removed / n_before * 100
# remove source_file and label columns
df <- df %>% dplyr::select(-dplyr::any_of(c("source_file","Label")))

# make key columns have the right types
df <- df %>%
  mutate(
    study_site  = as.character(study_site),
    plot_number = as.integer(plot_number),
    aru_number  = as.integer(aru_number),
    record_date = as.Date(record_date),
    record_hour = as.integer(record_hour)
  )
```
#### play around with the dataframe
```{r}
# When are calls most frequent? By record hour
hourly_counts <- df %>%
  filter(!is.na(record_hour), dplyr::between(record_hour, 0, 23)) %>%
  count(record_hour, name = "n") %>%
  complete(record_hour = 0:23, fill = list(n = 0)) %>%
  arrange(record_hour)
    # Line
    ggplot(hourly_counts, aes(x = record_hour, y = n)) +
      geom_line() +
      geom_point() +
      labs(title = "Detections by Hour (Line)", x = "Hour of Day (0–23)", y = "Count") +
      scale_x_continuous(breaks = 0:23) +
      theme_minimal()
    
# Which plots are high? By plot number
plot_counts <- df %>%
  filter(!is.na(plot_number)) %>%
  count(plot_number, name = "n") %>%
  arrange(desc(n))

ggplot(plot_counts, aes(x = reorder(factor(plot_number), n), y = n)) +
  geom_col() +
  geom_text(aes(label = n), hjust = -0.1, size = 3) +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(title = paste("Plots by Detections"),
       x = "Plot number", y = "Count") +
  theme_minimal()

# Which species dominate? By species
species_counts <- df %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(common_name), common_name != "") %>%
  dplyr::count(common_name, name = "n") %>%
  dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(n))
species_counts

# Daily frequency line by record date
daily_counts <- df %>%
  filter(!is.na(record_date)) %>%
  count(record_date, name = "n") %>%
  arrange(record_date)

ggplot(daily_counts, aes(x = record_date, y = n)) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_point() +
  labs(title = "Detections by Date", x = "Date", y = "Count") +
  theme_minimal()
```
#### Confidence level
```{r}
conf_cols <- intersect(c("Confidence","Confidence_source","Confidence_target"), names(df))
# Long format for shared summaries/plots
conf_long <- df %>%
  dplyr::select(all_of(conf_cols)) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "metric", values_to = "value")

# Summary table
conf_summary <- conf_long %>%
  group_by(metric) %>%
  summarise(
    n             = n(),
    n_non_missing = sum(!is.na(value)),
    n_na          = sum(is.na(value)),
    mean          = mean(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    median        = median(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd            = sd(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    min           = min(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    q05           = quantile(value, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE, type = 7),
    q25           = quantile(value, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE, type = 7),
    q75           = quantile(value, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE, type = 7),
    q95           = quantile(value, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE, type = 7),
    max           = max(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
conf_summary

# Box plot by metric
ggplot(conf_long, aes(x = metric, y = value)) +
  geom_boxplot(na.rm = TRUE) +
  labs(title = "Confidence Boxplots", x = "", y = "Value") +
  theme_minimal()
```
#### save the file
```{r}
clean_Hoh_ALL <- df
readr::write_csv(clean_Hoh_ALL, "E:/merge_all/Site/clean_Hoh_ALL.csv")
```
